title: Dichaelia filiformis
taxonomy: Dichaelia filiformis is classified within the family Boraginaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. The genus Dichaelia filiformis itself is part of the order Lamiales. The Boraginaceae family includes species that are primarily herbaceous, shrub-like, or tree-like. Dichaelia filiformis is a member of the plant kingdom, specifically classified as a flowering plant.

Dichanthium annulatum, also known as Dichelachne stenophylla or Dichelachne flexuosa, is a species of grass native to Australia and New Zealand. The leaves and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Dichanthium annulatum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dichanthium annulatum is often made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
